message:
Record frankenredis-hjnz AOF/RDB round-trip property test task in beads

Persist another newly-claimed beads issue into the JSONL audit log so
`br ready` and `bv --robot-triage` surface it on their next pass
instead of waiting for a manual flush later.

Issue details captured:

- id: frankenredis-hjnz
- title: "Add structure-aware AOF/RDB round-trip property tests to
  fr-persist"
- type: task, priority 2 (P2 / medium)
- status: in_progress (created_at and updated_at are ~6 seconds apart,
  so the task was opened and immediately claimed inside the same
  working session)
- source_repo: "."

The scope target is the existing `fr-persist` crate: structure-aware
round-trip coverage means the property generators emit legal AOF/RDB
opcode sequences (not random bytes), and the assertions compare the
decoded in-memory keyspace before and after a write/reload cycle
rather than byte-exact file equality. This is the natural complement
to the 70K-input fuzz coverage that the security epic already landed
(frankenredis-29c7 / frankenredis-9zk5).

Only the tracked `.beads/issues.jsonl` ledger moves in this commit.
No `fr-persist` source, fixtures, or CI config change. The file is
force-added because `.beads/` is gitignored while the JSONL itself is
tracked — the same pattern as every prior beads commit in this repo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenredis-hna","title":"[EPIC] Runtime-Configurable Database Count (NUM_DATABASES)","description":"## Overview

fr-store/src/lib.rs line 636 defines `pub const NUM_DATABASES: usize = 16`. This is used to size fixed-length arrays:
- db_key_counts: [usize; NUM_DATABASES]
- db_expires_counts: [usize; NUM_DATABASES]

Redis allows the database count to be configured at startup via the `databases` config parameter (default 16). Because NUM_DATABASES is a compile-time const used for array sizing, there is no way to change the database count at runtime.

## Why This Matters

While 16 is the standard default, some Redis deployments configure higher counts (e.g., 256). The config table advertises ("databases", "16") but this is purely cosmetic.

## Architecture Impact

This requires converting the fixed arrays to Vecs:
- `db_key_counts: Vec<usize>` initialized to vec![0; database_count]
- `db_expires_counts: Vec<usize>` initialized to vec![0; database_count]
- SELECT command validation: check against runtime database count, not const
- All encode_db_key/decode_db_key callers: validate db < database_count

## Risk

Medium — the fixed arrays are used in hot paths (key insertion/removal). Vec access is nearly identical in performance since the Vec is never resized after initialization. The main risk is missing a bounds check on database index somewhere.

## Sequencing

This should be done after the basic CONFIG SET wiring (frankenredis-kgn) is stable, since it's a structural change that touches the same code.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:25.566461636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:35:06.697215375Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:35:06.696359806Z","close_reason":"Epic acknowledged — children tasks now unblocked for implementation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
